description: The actual service that carries a communication channel, such as a Twilio number for SMS, a Telegram bot, or the site's email setup. Set one up to plug in provider credentials, limit which region or country it serves, and cap how many messages it sends per hour.

fields.description: Adapter that carries this channel. frappe-email = Frappe Email Account stack; in-app = portal inbox; telegram = Telegram bot; twilio = Twilio SMS/WhatsApp/Voice; print = generated PDF letters.
fields.label: Provider

fields.description: Routing scope: persons from this country prefer this account. Blank = default account for the channel.
fields.label: Country / Region

fields.description: Maximum sends per trailing hour through this account (free-tier throttle). 0 = unlimited. Emergency-category messages bypass the throttle.
fields.label: Hourly Limit

fields.description: Sending number in E.164 (e.g. +15551234567). For WhatsApp use the WhatsApp-enabled sender; the whatsapp: prefix is added automatically from the Channel. Leave blank if using a Messaging Service SID.
fields.label: From Number

fields.description: Optional. Use a Twilio Messaging Service (MG...) instead of a fixed From number.
fields.label: Messaging Service SID

fields.description: Auto-generated on save if left blank; Telegram echoes it back on each webhook call for verification. Stored encrypted.
fields.label: Webhook Secret

fields.description: Optional. The bot's @username (auto-detected from the token if blank).
fields.label: Bot Username

fields.description: Optional. The public HTTPS base/URL the provider was configured to call, if the site sits behind a proxy that rewrites the request URL (used for webhook signature verification).
fields.label: Public URL (override)

fields.description: Optional extra provider configuration as raw JSON, merged underneath the fields above (the typed fields win). Secrets belong in the encrypted fields above, not here.
fields.label: Extra Settings (JSON)

name: Channel Provider Account
